name: Blackrock
classification_notes: A chunky, display-oriented blackletter designed for headings and titles, placing it firmly in the blackletter category with a display-weight character.
summary: A single-weight chunky blackletter by Jeff Schreiber, designed for headings and titles.
description: Inspired by a blackletter workshop run by High on Type, Blackrock is a bold, chunky blackletter suited to display use. It comes in a single weight with full Latin coverage including diacritical marks and punctuation, available in WOFF, SVG, TTF and OTF formats. Licensing is tiered from free sample through personal to commercial use.
foundry_blurb: Regular Bold Italic describe Blackrock as a chunky blackletter that works well as a heading or title, noting its inspiration from a blackletter workshop run by their friends at High on Type.

name: Levo
classification_notes: A single-weight display script with bold, upright letterforms, characteristic straight handles on curves, and an extensive set of swashes and alternates — placing it firmly in the display script category.
summary: A display script designed with illustrator Rutger Paulusse, characterised by straight handles on curves and a generous set of swashes, alternates and ligatures.
description: Created in collaboration with graphic designer and illustrator Rutger Paulusse (Gwer), Levo is a single-weight display script defined by his signature treatment of straight handles on curved strokes. The family ships with swashes, alternates and ligatures that reward exploration, and covers uppercase, lowercase, punctuation, diacritics, numbers and symbols. Well suited to logo design, packaging and web headings where expressive lettering is needed.
foundry_blurb: Regular Bold Italic describe Levo as a collaboration with illustrator and graphic designer Rutger Paulusse, packed with flavour, bold characters and his signature straight handles on curves. The family includes several swashes, alternates and ligatures, and is recommended for logo design, packaging and web headings.

name: Staat
classification_notes: Staat features sharp terminal cuts, pointed serifs and high contrast strokes, placing it firmly in the modern or Didone-influenced serif tradition used primarily for display settings.
summary: A high-contrast display serif by Timo Kuilder, with pointed serifs and sharp terminal cuts, available in Bold and Bold Italic only.
description: Sharp terminal cuts and pointed serifs give this two-style family a modern, slightly severe character suited to headlines and display work. The basic Latin character set covers 310 glyphs including diacritics, ligatures and currency symbols, with OpenType features included. Tight spacing and kerning reinforce its precision, while deliberately shortened 'f' and 'j' descenders prevent gaps in web rendering.
foundry_blurb: Regular Bold Italic describe Staat as a high-contrast serif with sharp terminal cuts and pointy serifs, giving it a modern look and strong personality. It ships in Bold and Bold Italic with a 310-glyph basic Latin set, OpenType features and ligatures for rhythm optimisation, tightly spaced and kerned throughout. Web fonts are included in every download.

name: Barbour
classification_notes: A high-contrast stencil serif intended for large display use such as magazine headers and posters; the stencil cuts and dramatic thick-thin contrast place it firmly in display rather than body text territory.
summary: A single-weight stencil serif by Timo Kuilder, built around strong thick-thin contrast for editorial headlines and poster work.
description: Designed by Timo Kuilder, Barbour is a stencil serif with pronounced thick-thin contrast aimed at display-scale use. Its 213-glyph set covers uppercase, lowercase, diacritical marks, ligatures, currency and symbols, making it serviceable across most Western Latin editorial contexts. Best suited to magazine covers, posters and headings where the stencil interruptions and high contrast read as deliberate style rather than limitation.
foundry_blurb: Regular Bold Italic describe Barbour as a classy and elegant stencil serif with a luxurious feel, designed for editorial purposes including magazines, posters and covers. They note its big contrasts give it a lot of personality, and recommend it for headers rather than body text.

name: Arkiv
classification_notes: Described as a modern sans with bold character; clean geometric construction across three weights with no italics, fitting a geometric sans subcategory.
summary: A three-weight modern sans by Timo Kuilder with full Cyrillic support, marking Regular Bold Italic's first typeface to include the script.
description: Designed by Timo Kuilder, Arkiv covers Light, Regular and Bold weights with Latin and Cyrillic character sets, the latter developed with feedback from Philipp Nurullin. The family includes roman diacritical marks and some special alternates, available in WOFF, SVG, TTF and OTF formats. It sits in a bold, purposeful register suited to editorial and branding work requiring multilingual Latin-Cyrillic coverage.
foundry_blurb: Regular Bold Italic describe Arkiv as their first typeface family to include full Cyrillic characters, calling it a modern sans that is undeniably bold and up for any challenge. Designed by Timo Kuilder with Cyrillic glyph feedback from Philipp Nurullin, it comes in three weights with all roman diacritical marks and special alternates.

name: Rucksack
classification_notes: The foundry cites Futura as the primary inspiration, and the typeface is built from clean geometric shapes with a tall x-height, placing it firmly in the geometric sans tradition.
summary: A geometric sans in six weights by Jeff Schreiber, drawing from the Futura tradition with a tall x-height and slightly indented stems.
description: Built on clean geometric shapes with nods to Paul Renner's late-1920s Futura, Rucksack runs from Light to Black across six weights. The tall x-height aids legibility at text sizes, while the friendly character forms make it workable in display settings too. Available in WOFF, SVG, TTF and OTF formats, with webfont CSS included on download.
foundry_blurb: Regular Bold Italic describe Rucksack as a modern and versatile sans serif family with six weights from Light to Black, inspired primarily by 1920s geometric typefaces such as Futura. They highlight its clean shapes, friendly character, indented stems and tall x-height as features that serve both editorial print work and display applications.

name: Lucien
classification_notes: A condensed sans serif with rounded corners marketed primarily for display use, distinguishing it from text-oriented grotesque or humanist sans families.
summary: A condensed sans serif with rounded corners from Jeff Schreiber, offered in two weights and suited to display work.
description: The rounded terminals give Lucien a mild, approachable quality that separates it from harder-edged condensed display faces. Coverage extends to uppercase, lowercase, diacritical marks, punctuation and numbers, with files supplied as WOFF, SVG, TTF and OTF. With only two weights and no italic, the family is limited in scope but functional for short-form display and headline settings.
foundry_blurb: Regular Bold Italic describe Lucien as a versatile and modern condensed sans serif with a friendly character derived from its rounded corners, positioned for display purposes across a broad range of uses.

name: Gringo
classification_notes: A decorative serif with spiky stems drawing on wild west and western Americana visual traditions; designed explicitly as a display face rather than for text use.
summary: A single-weight decorative serif by Jeff Schreiber drawing on wild west vernacular, with spiky stems and an Americana sensibility.
description: Designed by Jeff Schreiber, Gringo takes its cues from the visual language of the American west: ranches, barbecue joints and bourbon labels. The spiky, detailed stems give the letterforms a rough, hand-crafted quality suited to display and branding work. Available in one weight with uppercase, small caps, punctuation, diacritics and numbers, distributed as WOFF, SVG, TTF and OTF.
foundry_blurb: Regular Bold Italic describe Gringo as a typeface inspired by the wild west, evoking barbecue joints, ranches and bourbon whiskey. It is a decorative serif distinguished by spiky detailed stems, sold under commercial and personal licence with a free sample available.

name: FatFrank
classification_notes: A single-weight geometric sans with rounded corners, drawing on early 20th century geometric sans-serif traditions in a bold, display-oriented style.
summary: A single-weight ultra black geometric sans with rounded corners, designed for large-scale display use.
description: Inspired by early 20th century geometric sans serifs, FatFrank is offered in one weight only: an ultra black cut with soft, rounded corners that gives it a friendly, approachable quality. It covers Latin and Cyrillic scripts with full punctuation and diacritical marks, and is available in WOFF, SVG, TTF and OTF formats. Works best at poster scale where its weight and rounded character can assert themselves.
foundry_blurb: Regular Bold Italic describe FatFrank as a bigboned and friendly typeface with rounded corners, inspired by early 20th century geometric sans serifs, and recommend it for use at larger print scales.

name: Razor
classification_notes: Razor is a display typeface built from thin multi-lined characters, placing it firmly in the decorative display category with an inline or ruled-line treatment typical of 1980s retro aesthetics.
summary: A single-weight inline display face by Jeff Schreiber evoking 1980s Miami aesthetics through thin, multi-lined letterforms.
description: Built around thin parallel-ruled characters, Razor calls up the neon-lit, pastel-saturated graphic language of 1980s Miami. It covers uppercase letters and numerals only, and works best at larger sizes where the multi-line detail reads clearly. Available as WOFF, SVG, TTF and OTF, the family ships with webfont CSS for straightforward web deployment.
foundry_blurb: Regular Bold Italic describe Razor as a typeface that takes you back to Miami in the 1980s, evoking pink flamingos, the stainless-steel DeLorean and synthesiser music. The thin multi-lined characters are recommended for use at larger sizes.

name: StackBill
classification_notes: A layerable geometric display typeface with three distinct overlay styles (regular, inline, back), intended primarily for headline and decorative use rather than body text.
summary: A geometric display typeface by Jeff Schreiber built around three stackable layers that can be combined to produce inline, shadowed or outlined effects.
description: Designed by Jeff Schreiber, StackBill is structured around three distinct layer styles — regular, inline and back — which can be overlaid in multiple combinations to create depth and decorative effects. The family covers uppercase and lowercase, punctuation, diacritical marks and numerals across four weights. Billmoney, a free sample, is included as part of the wider StackBill family.
foundry_blurb: Regular Bold Italic describe StackBill as a versatile and stackable typeface with a geometric structure, offering three different layers — regular, inline and back — that can be combined in multiple ways. Billmoney is available as a free sample from the family.
